commands.repair_reason: The PR needs contributor runtime proof plus a maintainer compatibility decision on whether Worktrunk is optional with fallback or mandatory with explicit upgrade docs.; Needs attention: the PR executes an undeclared Worktrunk binary from PATH as part of the local lifecycle.; Review finding: [P1] Preserve a fallback when Worktrunk is unavailable — package.json:38 - [P1] Keep dependency setup on the seed path — .confi... commands.review_summary:
The PR adds Worktrunk project config and `.worktreeinclude`, changes `dev:worktree` to `wt --yes dev`, delegates worktree dependency setup to Worktrunk hooks, and adjusts the local worktree helper process/env behavior.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection: the PR hard-calls `wt --yes dev` without adding Worktrunk to the package or lockfile, and it moves dependency prep to a Worktrunk hook while `seed:dev` stays outside that hook. I did not run the startup commands because this read-only review should not spawn local services.
commands.review_followup: The PR needs contributor runtime proof plus a maintainer compatibility decision on whether Worktrunk is optional with fallback or mandatory with explicit upgrade docs.
